title: AI Radar reviewed daily evidence snapshot - 2026-06-22
summary: As of the 2026-06-16 to 2026-06-22 public evidence window, the strongest reviewed signals cluster around enterprise AI rollout, developer-tool operations, SDK infrastructure changes, and verifiable agent research.
executive_summary: This reviewed local public report turns the existing evidence draft into a formal read-only report without running live model generation or writing to Supabase. It is intentionally scoped to the public evidence available in this repository snapshot: Samsung's enterprise ChatGPT/Codex rollout, OpenAI enterprise spend-control tooling, Anthropic SDK code-execution support, and recent arXiv work on device-cloud RAG and tool-grounded time-series agents.

sections.title: Enterprise AI deployment became the clearest market signal
sections.summary: The strongest product/business evidence in this window is enterprise adoption rather than a single model benchmark. Samsung's internal ChatGPT/Codex rollout and OpenAI's enterprise spend-control tooling point to operationalization pressure: buyers want both broad deployment and management controls.
bullets: Samsung's global ChatGPT Enterprise and Codex rollout is one of the strongest public signals that enterprise AI usage is moving from pilot to workforce-scale deployment.
OpenAI's spend controls and usage analytics indicate that cost governance is becoming a first-class enterprise AI product requirement.
caveats: Both enterprise signals come from OpenAI-owned public sources, so independent buyer-side validation remains important.
missing_evidence: Independent Samsung operator metrics and third-party enterprise adoption benchmarks are not available in this public evidence window.

sections.title: Developer and research evidence points toward operational reliability
sections.summary: The developer/research side of the window is less about consumer launches and more about reliability: SDK support for code execution, device-cloud RAG under document isolation, and tool-grounded reasoning for irregular time-series question answering.
bullets: Anthropic's Python SDK update adds code-execution support and reliability fixes, reinforcing SDKs as the operational surface for AI application builders.
CONCORD and IRTS-ToolBench show research attention moving toward verifiable, tool-grounded, privacy-aware agent and retrieval systems.
caveats: The arXiv evidence is research-stage and should not be read as production adoption.
missing_evidence: Runtime adoption data for the SDK and independent replication of the research results are not included.

citations.title: Samsung Electronics brings ChatGPT and Codex to employees
New usage analytics and updated spend controls for enterprises
CONCORD: Asynchronous Sparse Aggregation for Device-Cloud RAG under Document Isolation
Towards Verifiable Agentic Data Science: Solving Irregular TSQA Via Tool-Grounded Reasoning

caveats: This report is reviewed against the public evidence snapshot in the local repository and should not be presented as autonomous live monitoring.
The evidence window is not June 30 live data; freshness depends on the latest available public radar snapshot.
missing_evidence: Production adoption data for the research systems.
